description: Schema v2 dictionary: expert canonical shorthand is the compiler reference surface, primary_ms is the first official Malay human alias, and aliases are English pseudocode or compatibility spellings.

policy.beginner_statement_policy: BINA, PAPAR, and PULANG Malay aliases may be newline-terminated outside critical syntax; expert canonical shorthand and critical syntax remain semicolon-terminated.
policy.critical_boundary_policy: Memory, raw address, pointer, and hardware I/O syntax require explicit block and statement boundaries.

LET.description: Create an immutable typed or inferred binding.

MUT.description: Vocabulary marker for future mutable binding support.

THEN.description: Separate a condition from its branch body in expert canonical shorthand or alias surfaces.

WHILE.description: Historical provenance: Executable while-loop statement supported by parser, semantic analyzer, and code generator in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

LOOP.description: Historical provenance: Executable unconditional loop statement supported by parser, semantic analyzer, and code generator in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

LOOP_BREAK.description: Historical provenance: Executable loop-break control flow supported inside while and loop bodies in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

LOOP_CONTINUE.description: Historical provenance: Executable loop-continue control flow supported inside while and loop bodies in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

PRINT.description: Print an integer, boolean, string length, or address-shaped value.

HW.description: Vocabulary marker for hardware or freestanding-region declarations.

HW_ZONE.description: Open a lexical hardware I/O zone where raw address provenance is explicitly permitted.

ADDR.description: Address literal or address-shaped value marker.

USE.description: Import a standard or platform module contract.

RETURN.description: Return a value from a function body.

FFI.description: Vocabulary marker for future foreign-function interface declarations.

C_INTEROP.description: Vocabulary marker for future C ABI interop declarations.

RESOURCE.description: Vocabulary marker for future resource-scope declarations.

DROP.description: Vocabulary marker for future explicit resource-release operations.

UNSAFE.description: Historical provenance: Recognized vocabulary marker; parser emits an explicit unimplemented diagnostic in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

EXTERN.description: Historical provenance: Recognized vocabulary marker; parser emits an explicit unimplemented diagnostic in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

STRUCT.description: Historical provenance: Recognized vocabulary marker; parser emits an explicit unimplemented diagnostic in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

ENUM.description: Historical provenance: Recognized vocabulary marker; parser emits an explicit unimplemented diagnostic in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

STR.description: String type marker for future typed string declarations.

ASSIGN.description: Assignment delimiter in a binding or declaration.

SLASH.description: Numeric division operator; constant zero is rejected statically.

AND.description: Historical provenance: Executable logical AND operator supported by parser, semantic analyzer, and code generator in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

OR.description: Historical provenance: Executable logical OR operator supported by parser, semantic analyzer, and code generator in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

BIT_AND.description: Historical provenance: Executable bitwise AND operator supported by parser, semantic analyzer, and code generator in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

BIT_OR.description: Historical provenance: Executable bitwise OR operator supported by parser, semantic analyzer, and code generator in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

SHIFT_L.description: Historical provenance: Executable left shift operator supported by parser, semantic analyzer, and code generator in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

SHIFT_R.description: Historical provenance: Executable right shift operator supported by parser, semantic analyzer, and code generator in v1.21-alpha. Current behavior is governed by the active compiler and canonical validation.

LEFT_PAREN.description: Open grouped expression or parameter list.

RIGHT_PAREN.description: Close grouped expression or parameter list.

SEMICOLON.description: Statement separator; mandatory for expert and critical syntax.
